zh.lifeSummary: William Larkyn是Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn的兒子，於1445年出生在英國肯特。他於1470年與Joan Copeland在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Larkyn是Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn的儿子，于1445年出生在英国肯特。他于1470年与Joan Copeland在英国肯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Larkyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a medieval personal name, a diminutive of Lar-, Lor-, both pet forms of Lawrence , formed with the addition of the Middle English suffix -kin.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Lorcáin ‘descendant of Lorcán’, a personal name from a diminutive of lorc ‘fierce, cruel’. This Gaelic name was often regarded as an equivalent of English Lawrence .

variants.name: Lawrence
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Lorens, Laurence, from Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’, a place in Italy probably named from its laurels or bay trees. The name was borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Rome in the 3rd century AD ; he enjoyed a considerable cult throughout Europe, with consequent popularity of the personal name (French Laurent, Italian, Spanish Lorenzo, Catalan Llorenç, Portuguese Lourenço, German Laurenz, Polish Wawrzyniec, etc.). In Britain this is a common name from the 12th century, with pet forms such as Law , Low , Lawrie , Laurie , Larry , Larkin , all of which are represented in surnames. There was also a feminine form Laurencia which may have given rise to the English surname. The surname is also borne by Jews among whom it is presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Ashkenazic surnames. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Lorenz , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Slovenian Lavrenčič and Lovrenčič (patronymics from Lavrencij and Lovrenc, equivalents of Lawrence), Polish Wawrzyniak . Compare Larrance , Laurence , Lawerence , Lieurance , and Lowrance .

variants.name: Larkins
meanings: English: variant of Larkin 1, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: William Larkyn was born in 1445, in Kent, England as the son of Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn. He married Joan Copeland in 1470, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: William Larkyn est né en 1445, à Kent, Angleterre, fils de Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn. Il a épousé Joan Copeland en 1470, à Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: William Larkyn wurde 1445 in Kent, England, als Sohn von Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn geboren. Er hat 1470 in Kent, England Joan Copeland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: William Larkyn è nato il nel 1445 nel Kent, England come figlio di Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn. Lui sposato Joan Copeland nel 1470, nel Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: William Larkynは1445年にイギリスケントでSir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkynの息子として生まれた。1470年にイギリスケントでJoan Copelandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: William Larkyn(은)는 1445년 영국 켄트에서 Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1470년 영국 켄트에서 Joan Copeland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: William Larkyn nasceu em 1445, em Kent, Inglaterra filho de Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn. Ele casou-se com Joan Copeland em 1470, em Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: William Larkyn родился в 1445 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. Его отца звали Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Copeland в 1470 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: William Larkyn nació en 1445, en Kent, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Barthomew Lovekyn or Larkyn. Se casó con Joan Copeland en 1470, en Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
